## Client hierarchy

```
providers.Client
    Ôöé
    ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ retryConfig (types.RetryConfig)
    ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ circuitBreaker (*CircuitBreaker)
    ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ monitoring (*monitoring.System)
    ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ adapter (providerAdapter)   ÔćÉ wire format per provider
    ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ HTTP transport; delegates body/endpoint/headers/decode/stream
        to c.resolveAdapter() instead of switch c.provider

providers.Config
    ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ Routing
    Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ FallbackModels   []ModelIdentifier
    Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ FallbackProviders []APIProvider
    Ôöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ CircuitBreaker   *CircuitBreakerConfig
    ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ (per-provider options)
```

### Wire-format adapters

The per-provider wire format lives behind the `providerAdapter` interface
(`internal/providers/adapter.go`), replacing the former `switch c.provider`
statements in `client.go`:

| Adapter | Providers | Endpoint | Auth |
|---|---|---|---|
| `anthropicAdapter` | anthropic, bedrock, vertex, workers-ai, unknown (default) | `/v1/messages` | `x-api-key` + `anthropic-version` |
| `foundryAdapter` | foundry | `/v1/messages` | `api-key` + `Anthropic-Foundry-Resource-Id` |
| `openAICompatAdapter` | openai, z-ai, minimax, openrouter, mistral | `/chat/completions` | `Authorization: Bearer` |
| `codexAdapter` | codex | `/responses` | `Authorization: Bearer` + codex UA |
| `ollamaAdapter` | ollama | `/api/chat` | `Authorization: Bearer` (optional) |
| `geminiAdapter` | gemini | `:generateContent` / `:streamGenerateContent?alt=sse` | `Authorization: Bearer` |

Each adapter exposes `buildRequestBody`, `modelEndpoint`, `requestEndpoint`,
`applyAuthHeaders`, `decodeResponse`, and `createStreamResult`. **Adding a
provider** means reusing an existing adapter (most OpenAI-compatible gateways
just need a base URL/model entry) or implementing a small one, then mapping it
in `adapterForProvider` ÔÇö no edits to `client.go` switches. The canonical
message format stays Anthropic-shaped (`internal/types`); adapters translate
only at the network edge. Per-provider behaviour is pinned by golden tests in
`internal/providers/adapter_test.go`.

---

## Retry strategy

Two retry layers operate independently:

### HTTP-level retry (`retry.RetryHTTP`)

Handles network and HTTP errors directly on the transport:

```
Attempt 1 Ôćĺ send
  ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ 200 OK Ôćĺ return
  ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ 429 Too Many Requests Ôćĺ read Retry-After header or backoff Ôćĺ retry
  ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ 503/504/500/408/409 Ôćĺ backoff Ôćĺ retry
  ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ ClientError (4xx) / AuthError (401/403) Ôćĺ no retry, return error
  ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ Network error Ôćĺ backoff Ôćĺ retry

Max attempts: configured in types.RetryConfig (default 3)
Backoff: exponential with ┬▒25% jitter, capped at MaxBackoff
```

### Loop-level recovery (`loop.tryRecovery`)

Handles `ErrCodeAPIRateLimit` and `ErrCodeAPITimeout` at the loop level:

```
model call fails with recoverable error
  Ôöé
  ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ isRecoverableError? (structured code or network classifier)
  Ôöé     ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ yes Ôćĺ tryRecovery()
  Ôöé     Ôöé         ÔÇó linear backoff: multiplier ├Ś attempt
  Ôöé     Ôöé         ÔÇó max: MaxOutputTokensRecoveryLimit attempts
  Ôöé     Ôöé         ÔÇó continue loop on success
  Ôöé     ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ no  Ôćĺ return error, loop terminates
```

### Error classification

`retry.ClassifyHTTPError(err, statusCode)` returns one of:

| Classification | Retried? |
|---|---|
| `Network` | Ôťô |
| `RateLimit` | Ôťô |
| `ServerOverload` | Ôťô |
| `Timeout` | Ôťô |
| `ServerError` | Ôťô |
| `ClientError` | ÔťŚ |
| `AuthError` | ÔťŚ |
| `Unknown` | Ôťô |

`shouldRetryWithStrategy` also checks `EngineError.IsPermanent()` ÔÇö auth, invalid-input, and permission errors are never retried.

---

## Circuit breaker

```go
type CircuitBreaker struct {
    state           State  // Closed | Open | HalfOpen
    failureCount    int
    successCount    int
    lastStateChange time.Time
    config          *CircuitBreakerConfig
}
```

| Config field | Default | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `FailureThreshold` | 5 | Consecutive failures to open |
| `SuccessThreshold` | 2 | Consecutive successes to close from half-open |
| `Timeout` | 60s | Open Ôćĺ half-open transition delay |

Wrap any client with `client.EnableCircuitBreaker()` or supply a custom config with `client.EnableCircuitBreakerWithConfig(cfg)`.

---

## Fallback routing

Configure via `providers.Config`:

```go
cfg := &providers.Config{
    Routing: &providers.RoutingConfig{
        FallbackModels: []types.ModelIdentifier{
            {Provider: "anthropic", Model: "claude-haiku-4-5"},
        },
        FallbackProviders: []types.APIProvider{
            types.APIProviderBedrock,
        },
        CircuitBreaker: &providers.CircuitBreakerConfig{
            FailureThreshold: 3,
        },
    },
}
```

The loop tries fallback models first (same provider, cheaper/faster model), then fallback providers (different endpoint), before giving up.

---

## Streaming

`client.CreateMessageStreamResultWithCallback(ctx, req, onChunk)` streams response chunks:

```go
type APIResponseChunk struct {
    Type         APIChunkType  // content_block_start | delta | stop | message_stop | error
    DeltaType    string        // "text_delta" | "input_json_delta"
    Delta        string        // text fragment
    PartialJSON  string        // partial tool input JSON
    ContentBlock ContentBlock  // for content_block_start
    StopReason   *string
}
```

Each chunk is forwarded to:
1. `ResponseChunkCallback` (if set on session) ÔÇö synchronous callback in the loop goroutine
2. `EventQueue` (always present on session) ÔÇö buffered async channel, overflow drops silently

---

## Adding a new provider

1. Add the provider constant to `types/api.go:APIProvider`
2. Add credential/endpoint handling in `providers/config.go`
3. Implement client logic (HTTP request building, response parsing) in `providers/individual/`
4. Register in `providers.NewFallbackClient()`
5. Add to the provider info table in `providers/registry.go`
